name: UChicago 3 Week Immersion
session_dates: Summer (3 weeks, residential on UChicago campus)

cost.notes: $9,300 residential. Chicagoland area commuters: $6,100. Need-based financial aid available for students with household income under $125,000.

prerequisites.grade_range: Grades 9-11 (must be at least 14 years old)
prior_knowledge: Varies by course; some STEM courses have prerequisites listed in course descriptions

essays.prompt: Why are you interested in this particular Immersion course topic?
tips: UChicago values intellectual curiosity and depth of engagement — show specific, informed interest in the subject area

outcomes.college_impact: UChicago's 3 Week Immersion provides college-level credit and a taste of the UChicago academic experience. Well-regarded for students targeting top research universities.
outcomes.typical_results: Students earn 100 units of UChicago undergraduate credit. Alumni often cite the program as formative for their academic direction.

what_they_look_for: Academic preparedness appropriate to the specific course
Intellectual maturity and readiness for undergraduate-level coursework
Genuine interest in exploring a topic in depth

blurbs.short: A 3-week residential undergraduate-level course at the University of Chicago for high school students in grades 9-11. STEM tracks include Computer Science/Data Science/Math/Statistics, Physical Sciences, Biological Sciences, and Technology/Molecular Engineering. Students earn 100 units of UChicago credit.
blurbs.long: The UChicago 3 Week Immersion is a rigorous 3-week residential program where high school students take a single undergraduate-level course on the UChicago campus. The program is open to students in grades 9-11 (must be at least 14 years old), and all participants live in campus housing. STEM tracks include: Computer Science, Data Science, Math, and Statistics; Biological Sciences; Physical Sciences (astronomy, physics); Technology (Computer Science, Molecular Engineering); and Psychology and Neuroscience. Courses are taught by UChicago faculty and carry 100 units of undergraduate credit (equivalent to one full quarter-long course). The program costs $9,300 residential or $6,100 for Chicagoland commuters. Need-based financial aid is available for students from households earning under $125,000. UChicago's distinctive academic approach — emphasis on intellectual rigor, inquiry, and cross-disciplinary thinking — is fully on display in these programs.

curriculum: 3 weeks of intensive undergraduate-level coursework in the chosen discipline; workshop discussions; research projects; hands-on activities; residential college life on the UChicago campus.
